title: Customer Experience Virtual Assistant (Phone Based)
description:
Note: Interested applicants must submit their application exclusively through the OnlineJobs platform. Do not
 the company directly. Any candidate who contacts the company outside of the OnlineJobs system will be disqualified from consideration.



Are you someone who takes pride in handling customer interactions the right way—calmly, professionally, and consistently? Do you do well with clear expectations, scripts, and processes, and enjoy improving quickly through feedback? If so, Maid In America Cleaning Services, based in Clarksville, TN, wants to hear from you.



We’re seeking a Customer Experience Virtual Assistant to serve as a frontline point of contact for our customers and a dependable executor of structured processes. Working closely with our sales and customer experience teams, you’ll be responsible for answering inbound calls, handling customer requests, following defined scripts, and routing issues accurately.



This role is process-driven and phone-heavy, especially during training. The work can be fast-paced at times, but it is highly structured. Your ability to stay calm, follow instructions precisely, and take coaching seriously will have a direct impact on customer satisfaction and team efficiency.



What You’ll Do



- Answer & Handle Inbound Customer Calls: Be the first point of contact for customers and new inquiries, answering calls promptly, professionally, and calmly while following provided phone scripts.

- Follow Scripts & Decision Trees: Handle common customer needs—such as scheduling questions, reschedules, basic service inquiries, and initial issue intake—by following clear scripts and escalation rules exactly.

- Route Requests Accurately: Determine when a request can be handled directly and when it should be escalated to the appropriate tea
mber, ensuring clean handoffs and no dropped balls.

- Create a Positive Customer Experience: Make customers feel heard, cared for, and confident that their request is being handled, even when issues or complaints arise.

- Document Interactions & Updates: Log accurate notes and updates in our systems so the internal team always has clear context and next steps.

- Support Structured Administrative Workflows: Execute defined backend processes (task tracking, updates, file movement, and follow-through) by following SOPs and checklists without skipping steps.

- Receive Coaching & Improve Quickly: Actively apply feedback on calls, scripts, and workflows, improving speed, accuracy, and confidence over time.



Who We’re Looking For



- Process-Driven & Detail-Oriented: You’re comfortable following scripts, checklists, and SOPs exactly, and you don’t skip steps or “freestyle” your work.

- Calm & Professional on the Phone: You communicate clearly and confidently, stay composed with customers, and can handle questions or mild frustration without getting flustered.

- Highly Coachable: You take feedback seriously, apply it quickly, and improve fast—especially when it comes to phone scripts and customer handling.

- Reliable & Consistent: You show up on time, follow through on tasks, and can be counted on to do things the right way every time.

- Comfortable with Structure: You prefer clear expectations, defined processes, and knowing exactly what “good” looks like in your role.

- Tech-Comfortable: You’re comfortable working inside tools like Slack, shared documents, and task systems—or you can learn them quickly with instructions.

- Strong Communicator: Whether by phone, chat, or written notes, you communicate clearly, professionally, and respectfully.



Our Core Values



- Communication

- Connection

- Quality

- Service

- Teamwork



If you share these values, we want you on our team to help drive our company forward.



Important Note: Screening Step



SERIOUS APPLICANTS ONLY:

Complete our personality test to see if there’s a mutual fit:


/p/fzxN5FC9302s 

Give honest answers; there are no right or wrong results.



Submit your application with the subject line beginning with the core value we’ve listed that you most resonate with.



IMPORTANT: When applying, please use AT LEAST 5 Apply Points



Skills & Requirements

- Fluent English: Strong spoken and written English is required. This is a phone-based role, and customers must be able to understand you clearly and comfortably.

- Customer Service Experience: Prior experience handling customer inquiries or support requests over the phone is required.

- Process & Administrative Competence: You’re comfortable following multi-step processes, documenting information accurately, and keeping systems up to date.

- Attention to Detail: You consistently capture the right information, follow instructions precisely, and avoid careless errors.

- Coachability: You’re open to feedback, willing to be corrected, and able to apply coaching quickly—especially when learning phone scripts and customer-handling expectations.

- Technical Readiness:

  - Reliable computer with backup power options

  - Quality headset and microphone

  - Quiet workspace suitable for phone calls

- Ability to Work Monday-Friday, 8AM-5PM CST.

- Reliable Internet & Backup Options: Stable internet connection with a backup plan to ensure consistent availability and responsiveness.



Why Join Maid In America Cleaning Services?



- Impactful Role: Your quick execution and initiative will directly affect customer satisfaction and operational efficiency.

- Growth-Oriented Culture: We embrace new ideas, reward proactive thinking, and train daily for continual improvement.

- Supportive Team: Collaborate with a group that values each other’s contributions and thrives on a feedback-rich environment.



If you’re passionate about productivity, eager to work in a fast-paced setting, and confident in your ability to deliver high-quality results quickly, apply now and become an integral part of the Maid In America family!



Apply Today: Submit your application per the instructions above. We look forward to learning more about you!

title: Project Manager - Amazon Marketing Agency
description:
Project Manager – Amazon Marketing Agency (Remote)



Full-Time | Remote | Competitive Salary + PTO



About Us:



At The Amazon Whisperer, we’re not your average agency. We’re a high-growth Amazon marketing agency focused on driving profitable sales growth for our clients through SEO, PPC, listing optimization, and catalog management. We work fast, think strategically, and support one another like a true team. Now, we’re looking for a detail-oriented and driven Project Manager to help lead and organize our growing internal team.



About the Role:



As our Project Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role in ensuring smooth day-to-day operations across up to 10 tea
mbers. You’ll oversee project timelines, assign tasks, and keep everyone accountable using Asana and Slack. You’ll also be the support system for your team ensuring they have what they need, jumping in to assist where necessary, and tracking performance through tools like Hubstaff and reporting spreadsheets.



What You’ll Do:



- Assign and track tasks in Asana to ensure deadlines are met

- Lead weekly team calls and provide performance updates to the COO

- Monitor team productivity and efficiency using Hubstaff

- Communicate with tea
mbers via Slack to provide daily support

- Identify team skill gaps and help develop training plans

- Update project and performance reporting spreadsheets

- Act as the operational backbone, ensuring nothing slips through the cracks



What You’ll Need:



- 2+ years Amazon experience (catalog management, SEO, etc.)

- Experience in a fast-paced agency environment

- Strong organizational and communication skills

- Fast and reliable internet connection

- Ability to type 55+ words per minute

- Comfortable using Google Sheets/Excel

- Fluent in English (written and spoken)



Company Culture:



At The Amazon Whisperer, we highly value transparency, communication, and trust. This is not just another job—it’s a place where your ideas are valued, hard work is rewarded, and growth opportunities are everywhere. We’re building a team that operates like a unit, supportive, proactive, and relentlessly focused on client success. If you’re looking for a long-term remote role where you can grow your skills and career, you’re in the right place.



What You’ll Get:



- Competitive pay

- 13 days PTO, including public holidays

- 100% remote work

- Room to grow with the company

- A passionate, supportive team that values your voice and ideas



Apply today and be part of a company that’s scaling fast and doing things differently in the Amazon space. We’re not looking for average. We’re looking for exceptional.



Submit your application here:

title: PPC Specialist – Amazon Marketing Agency
description:
PPC Specialist – Amazon Marketing Agency (Remote)



Full-Time | Remote | Competitive Salary + PTO



About Us:



The Amazon Whisperer is a fast-growing, full-service Amazon marketing agency that delivers profitable sales growth for consumer brands. We specialize in Amazon SEO, PPC, listing optimization, and catalog management, and we do it with systems, strategy, and speed. We’re looking for a PPC Specialist who wants to grow into a world-class media buyer by executing and managing high-performing Amazon ad campaigns.



The Role:



The PPC Specialist plays a hands-on role in the day-to-day execution of Amazon advertising campaigns. You’ll be responsible for uploading bulk files, adjusting bids and budgets, building campaigns, harvesting keywords, and ensuring PPC performance stays aligned with ACoS, TACoS, and budget targets. This is a perfect fit for someone who wants to master the technical side of Amazon ads and learn how to run efficient, scalable campaigns from the ground up.



You’ll work directly under the PPC Lead, taking direction on strategy while owning the implementation across multiple client accounts.



Responsibilities:



- Work closely with the PPC Lead to implement strategies across client accounts

- Create new campaigns (SP, SB, SD) and restructure existing ones for efficiency

- Adjust bids, update budgets, and modify placement settings

- Harvest high-performing keywords and promote them into new campaigns

- Apply negative keyword targeting to eliminate wasted ad spend

- Track and maintain campaign health daily to ensure accounts remain within budget

- Monitor and flag issues with ACoS, TACoS, or unexpected spend spikes

- Harvest PPC data to support organic SEO initiatives

- Maintain clean data and reports in spreadsheets and tracking tools



Requirements:



- Minimum 2 years of Amazon PPC experience (SEO knowledge is a plus)

- Proficiency in Helium 10, Data Dive, Jungle Scout, or similar keyword tools

- Strong analytical mindset and comfort working with performance data

- Must be proficient in Google Sheets and Excel

- Experience using automated ad platforms like Trellis, Adlabs, Bidx, Teikametrics, Quartile, Perpetua, or other similar sofware

- Clear communication skills for working with team leads and managers

- Reliable and organized, able to manage multiple campaigns without hand-holding

- Fast and reliable internet connection

- Fluent English (written and spoken)

- Ability to type 55+ words per minute



Company Culture:



At The Amazon Whisperer, we highly value transparency, communication, and trust. This is not just another job, it’s a place where your ideas are valued, hard work is rewarded, and growth opportunities are everywhere. We’re building a team that operates like a unit. Supportive, proactive, and relentlessly focused on client success. If you’re looking for a long-term remote role where you can grow your skills and career, you’re in the right place.



What You’ll Get:



- Competitive pay

- 13 days PTO (including public holidays)

- 100% remote work

- Opportunity to grow into roles like PPC Lead and beyond

- A collaborative and growth-minded team environment



Apply today and be part of a company that’s scaling fast and doing things differently in the Amazon space. We’re not looking for average. We’re looking for exceptional.



Submit your application here:

title: Manager Operations Analytics & Systems
description:
Manager, Operations Analytics & Systems



Only applicants who meet the required experience will be considered. Please highlight your relevant experience in your application. Submission of a CV/resume is required to be considered for this position. Applications without a resume will not be considered 



Role Overview

This role is responsible for overseeing the company’s analytics, systems, and workflow functions as a unified operational capability. The Manager ensures that data, tools, and processes are aligned, scalable, and designed to support efficient decision-making across the organization.

This position manages technical and analytical tea
mbers, sets standards, defines priorities, and acts as the connective layer between leadership, operations, and execution teams. As the organization grows, this role expands naturally to oversee additional analytics, systems, or process-oriented roles.

Core Responsibilities

Team Leadership and Direction

Manage and mentor analysts, engineers, and systems-focused tea
mbers

Set priorities, timelines, and deliverables across analytics and systems work

Ensure clear ownership, accountability, and quality across all outputs

Review work for accuracy, usability, and alignment with business objectives

Analytics and Reporting Oversight

Own the company’s operational and workforce reporting strategy

Define and maintain standard metrics, definitions, and source-of-truth logic

Ensure dashboards and reports are actionable, consistent, and trusted

Translate leadership questions into structured analytical workstreams

Validate assumptions, calculations, and interpretations before distribution

Systems and Workflow Governance

Oversee the design and evolution of internal systems and workflows

Ensure tools are configured intentionally and work well together

Approve major workflow changes, automations, and integrations

Maintain consistency in how data flows across platforms

Prevent tool sprawl and fragmented processes

Cross Functional Alignment

Serve as the bridge between leadership, operations, analytics, and technical teams

Gather requirements and convert them into clear execution plans

Ensure insights from analytics are reflected in system or workflow changes

Support stakeholders in understanding and using tools and reports effectively

Documentation and Standards

Ensure documentation exists for systems, workflows, and reporting assets

Establish standards for naming, access, permissions, and maintenance

Reduce reliance on institutional knowledge held by individuals

Continuous Improvement and Scale

Identify recurring inefficiencies across tools, data, and workflows

Drive ongoing improvements based on usage patterns and feedback

Prepare systems and analytics functions to scale as the organization grows

Support onboarding of future roles within analytics, systems, or operations

What Success Looks Like

Leadership consistently relies on reporting and systems for decisions

Metrics are clearly defined and universally understood

Tools and workflows reduce manual effort and operational friction

Analytics insights translate into concrete operational actions

The organization scales without breaking processes or visibility

Qualifications

Experience managing analytics, systems, or operations-focused teams

Strong understanding of operational data, reporting, and performance metrics

Strong systems thinking and workflow design capability

Ability to translate business needs into structured technical and analytical work

Excellent communication, prioritization, and organizational skills

Ideal Candidate Traits

Thinks holistically about how data, tools, and people interact

Comfortable balancing strategic oversight with practical execution

Values clarity, consistency, and scalability

Enjoys building durable systems rather than one-off solutions

Able to grow with the organization as scope and responsibility expand

Equal Opportunity Employer

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and strongly value diversity. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, religion, gender, age, disability, sexual orientation, or any other protected status.

title: Agile Functional Tester (Web, API & End-to-End)
description:
Are you a detail-obsessed QA tester who loves catching bugs before they reach production?

Do you thrive in an Agile team environment where your work directly impacts product quality and user trust?

We’re looking for a QA Functional Tester to join a Canadian FINTECH company through L’EVATE Inc., a staffing partner connecting top Filipino talent with global businesses.



Why This Role is Exciting

This isn’t just another QA job. You’ll be working on web platforms, APIs, and backend systems that power real-world financial solutions. Your testing expertise will ensure our applications are reliable, scalable, and user-friendly.

You’ll collaborate with developers, product owners, and automation testers — helping shape quality standards across the entire SDLC.



What You’ll Do

• Design, execute, and maintain manual test cases for web apps, APIs, and backend systems

• Perform functional, integration, regression, and exploratory testing

• Ensure traceability between user stories, test cases, and defects

• Run end-to-end test scenarios and validate data integrity

• Log and manage defects using Jira/Azure DevOps

• Join Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, daily standups, retrospectives)

• Communicate risks and testing progress with clear reports



What We’re Looking For

• 3–6 years QA functional testing experience (manual, web + API)

• Strong understanding of Agile SDLC (Scrum/Kanban)

• Skilled at writing clear and comprehensive test cases

• Experience with Postman, Swagger, or Fiddler for API testing

• Familiar with defect tracking in Jira or Azure DevOps

• Excellent communication skills and attention to detail

• Ability to work EST Time Zone



Nice-to-Haves

• Degree/Diploma in Computer Science, IT, or related

• Basic SQL knowledge (data validation)

• Exposure to performance, security, or accessibility testing

• Knowledge of automation concepts



What’s In It for You

• Be part of a Top Canadian FINTECH company

• Long-term role with career growth opportunities

• Competitive compensation based on skills & experience

• 100% remote, Philippines-based



How to Apply (Follow Instructions Exactly)

To make sure we find the best testers, please apply with:

1. Subject Line: QA Functional Tester – [Your Full Name]

2. Resume/CV – Your updated version

3.. Optional (Bonus): If you’ve used Postman/Swagger, send a screenshot of a test request you’ve written.



**Due to a high number of applicants, only those shortlisted will be contacted**



Apply Today

If you’re passionate about quality, collaboration, and problem-solving, we want you on our team. Apply now and take the next step in your QA career with a leading Canadian FINTECH!

title: Record Label Internship (Artist Growth & Promotion)
description:

About the Internship

We’re an AI-focused record label building and developing AI artists—and we’re looking for interns to help grow fanbases, increase streams, and secure brand opportunities through smart, consistent online promotion. This is a task-based internship with clear performance goals and a pathway to a permanent salary.





Company Structure

Interns will collaborate within Core Mind Technology’s AI, automation, and digital growth ecosystem while supporting Jennings Global Entertainment’s AI music roster.



Internship Type

Task-Based (complete assigned growth tasks and deliverables)

Remote / Online

Growth-focused and performance-drivenInternship Nature

This is a performance-driven internship, not a traditional hourly role.

Interns are evaluated based on measurable growth outcomes (streams, engagement, audience growth, and monetization).

Tasks are structured, assigned, and tracked to ensure clarity and accountability.



What You’ll Do

title: Ecommerce video editor
description:
Hey — I’m Karos. I’m 17, and I’m running a 7-figure e-commerce brand. This is not a corporate, LinkedIn-style job post. No fluff. No buzzwords. No BS. I’m looking for a full-time Video Editor who understands direct response and actually cares about performance. This is not a “just cut clips together” role. This is about making ads that perform.



I’m building fast and I’m obsessed with results. This is only the beginning. If you join now, you’re not just editing videos — you’re growing with me, long-term. This is NOT a short-term gig. It’s a chance to build something real together.



What you’ll be doing:

Editing direct-response video ads (Meta-focused)

Creating high-converting ads, not just aesthetic edits

Producing 3 hooks per video

Turning our raw ideas + strategy into scroll-stopping creatives

Using AI tools like ElevenLabs and AI Personas where it makes sense

Iterating based on performance data

Working directly with me — fast feedback, fast decisions

Helping push the brand from “doing well” to dominating



VERY Highly valued skill:

- AI: Experience with tools like HeyGen, Google Veo, Kling, Nano Banana

Candidates with this skill set will have a strong advantage.



Who I’m looking for:

You’ve edited direct-response ads for e-commerce brands

You understand performance, not just visuals

You have a STRONG EYE for details

You’re fast, disciplined, and competitive

You care about results and high standards

You want to work exclusively on one brand and help grow it

If you want something slow, safe, and corporate — this isn’t for you.



Compensation:

Full-time, remote role

Base salary depends on experience and skill

Performance-based bonuses tied directly to ad results

For strong performers, monthly bonuses can realistically reach around $1,000, depending on output and performance. There’s no fixed cap — results drive upside. If you make winning ads, you make more money. When the brand wins, you win.



The Big opportunity:

This starts as a Video Editor role, but there’s room to grow. If you consistently deliver, understand what’s working, and show good judgment over time, you can move into a better role with more responsibility and higher pay. I’m building brands for the long run and looking for people who want stability, ownership, and multi-year collaboration, not constant job hopping.



Application process (important):

To filter unserious applicants, start your message with:

 “I create high-converting creatives.”

Then include:

Your experience with video editing & direct response ads

Whether you have AI experience

Links to relevant e-commerce ad work (portfolio)

Your availability and start date

Why you want to work with us



If this resonates and you’d thrive in this environment, D
 and I’ll get back to you. 

Speak soon, Karos

Only applicants who meet the required experience will be considered. Please highlight your relevant experience in your application. Submission of a CV/resume is required to be considered for this position. Applications without a resume will not be considered 



Overview

We are looking for a Workflow Engineer to design, implement, and maintain the software systems that support our operations. This role focuses on improving productivity through better tools, smarter configurations, and clear, well-functioning workflows. You will collaborate with leadership and tea
mbers to understand needs, eliminate inefficiencies, and create systems that scale as the company grows.



Key Responsibilities



Software and Systems Analysis



• Audit all current software platforms and identify gaps, redundancies, and inefficiencies

• Evaluate team workflows to determine where tools are underutilized or misaligned

• Create clear recommendations for improvement based on business needs



Tool Selection and Implementation



• Research and recommend new tools that increase operational productivity

• Lead the setup and configuration of new software

• Build integrations between platforms to reduce manual steps and prevent errors

• Configure automations, workflows, access controls, and permissions



Workflow Optimization



• Translate existing business processes into streamlined digital workflows

• Build systems that support repeatable, scalable operations

• Document configurations and workflow logic for ongoing reference



Team Training and Adoption



• Train tea
mbers on new tools, workflows, and best practices

• Provide clear guidance to ensure proper usage of software across roles

• Create reference materials that support strong adoption and reduce knowledge gaps



Continuous Improvement



• Monitor system performance and user behavior to identify improvement opportunities

• Adjust workflows as business needs evolve

• Stay current on new features, emerging tools, and best practices in workflow automation



What Success Looks Like



• Teams use software consistently and correctly

• Manual tasks are reduced through well-designed automations

• Workflows run smoothly with fewer errors, delays, or handoff issues

• The company operates with clear, documented systems that scale cleanly

• Tools support growth rather than slow it down



Qualifications



• Experience configuring or administering business software systems

• Strong understanding of workflow design, automation, and integration concepts

• Ability to evaluate tools and match them to operational needs

• Comfortable learning new platforms quickly

• Strong communication skills and the ability to explain technical concepts clearly

• Proven ability to collaborate across departments



Preferred Skills



• Experience with workflow or automation tools such as Zapier, Make, Power Automate, or similar

• Familiarity with project management or operations platforms such as ClickUp, Monday, or Asana

• Ability to map processes visually using flowchart tools

• Strong problem solving and systems thinking ability



Ideal Candidate Traits



• Enjoys building order from complexity

• Curious and resourceful in finding the best tools for the job

• Strong attention to detail and quality

• Comfortable running training sessions and supporting user adoption

• Thinks in systems and understands how tools and people interact

title: Executive Assistant to the CEO/Co-Founder
description:
About the role

We’re looking for someone to assist with reducing friction and leveraging priorities that rotate between customer and partner work, product and planning, go-to-market, fundraising and investor relations, and conference-heavy periods.

You will own be responsible for assisting with the operating cadence of the founder between calendar management, meeting preparation and follow-through, travel and logistics, and a lightweight system for decisions and action items.

This is an execution-focused role with high trust, high context, and may have opportunity for increasing scope over time.

Outcomes you will own

• Calendar management: Meetings have clear agendas, pre-reads, links, and the right people, and there is protected time for deep work and strategic thinking when the prior week has been meeting-compressed. 

• Follow-ups. Action items, owners, deadlines, and next steps are captured and follow-up for completion across customer work, internal team cadence, and external stakeholders.

• Information is easy to find. Decisions, notes, and key documents are organized and linked (especially around high-stakes threads like fundraising, contracts, and customer pilots).

• Admin load stays low even during travel and conference surges. Logistics, scheduling, and communications remain clean and predictable during ramp periods. 



Key responsibilities

1) Calendar & time management

• Own calendar end-to-end: scheduling, rescheduling, prioritization, and conflict resolution.

• Build a weekly cadence to match working rhythm: 

o Meeting-dense days supported with frictionless prep.

o At least one protected strategic-thinking block per week when needed. 

• Ensure meeting hygiene: 

o Agenda and “decisions needed” are explicit.

o Pre-reads and relevant links are attached directly to invites. 

• Coordinate across internal tea
mbers and external stakeholders (customers, partners, investors).

2) Meeting preparation, notes, and follow-through

• Before meetings: 

o Assemble a one-page briefing: context, goals, stakeholders, links, and open questions.

• During/after meetings: 

o Capture decisions, action items, and owners.

o Publish a short recap and drive follow-ups.

• Maintain a lightweight decision trail using Shelby’s Decision Log approach, so key decisions and delegations are easy to track and execute.

3) Admin and operations support (founder support)

Based on recurring work patterns in task lists and meeting cadences, you will support admin across:

• Fundraising / investor relations admin (scheduling, document coordination, follow-ups, tracking next steps). 

• Contracts and document workflows (organizing drafts, routing for review, tracking signatures and next actions).

• Finance admin support (invoice organization, payment reminders, coordinating with vendors and advisors).

• Conference and event logistics (travel booking, itineraries, meeting scheduling around events, follow-ups after events).

4) Travel and logistics

• Plan end-to-end travel: flight options, buffers, hotels, ground transport, meeting clustering, and itineraries.

• Build travel packs that make it easy to execute on the road (contacts, confirmations, local logistics, key docs).

5) Systems & continuous improvement

• Improve the “EA dashboard” workflow over time: templates, checklists, and SOPs that make recurring tasks repeatable.

• Proactively spot gaps, reduce back-and-forth, and propose better ways to run the operating cadence.



What success looks like (30/60/90)

First 30 days

• Establish a clean calendar system and meeting-prep workflow.

• Implement a consistent post-meeting recap + action-item tracking habit.

• Build a baseline weekly rhythm that protects deep work time. 

60 days

• Travel and conference logistics run smoothly with minimal Shelby input.

• Decision and follow-up tracking is reliable and visible (no dropped balls).

90 days

• You independently manage the operating cadence: calendar strategy, meeting pipelines, follow-ups, and recurring admin.

• Founder's time is noticeably “higher quality”: fewer context switches, fewer logistics tasks, clearer priorities.



Ideal candidate profile

• Extremely organized and detail-strong, but not rigid. You can handle rapid changes and shifting priorities.

• High agency and proactive follow-through. You do not wait to be asked twice.

• Excellent written communication. You can write crisp recaps,
s, and briefs.

• Strong judgment and discretion. You are comfortable handling sensitive information.

• Systems thinker. You enjoy building simple processes that keep things moving.

• Comfortable working in Notion (or excited to learn quickly), Microsoft suite, and Hubspot are added benefits.

at least three years of experience in customer service, specifically within the e-commerce or dropshipping sectors. title: Sales Manager / Client Acquisition Director (High-Ticket Advisory Sales)
description:
About Our Firm



Harvest AF is built on the belief that financial knowledge should be used to serve others.



We believe people should be good stewards of the resources they have been given. Our mission is to help business owners and families keep more of what they earn, eliminate unnecessary taxes, and build generational stability for their households.



Unlike traditional tax firms that only prepare tax returns, our firm focuses on:

• tax strategy

• tax planning

• business advisory

• financial clarity for business owners



We work with clients across the United States and regularly help them implement strategies such as:

• S-Corporation optimization

• retirement tax strategies

• real estate tax strategies

• depreciation planning

• entity structuring

• business tax optimization

Our mission is to build a high-trust advisory firm that helps business owners keep more of what they earn while building generational wealth.



Role Overview



We are hiring a Sales Manager who will be responsible for closing advisory clients and managing our sales pipeline.

This role is not a call center position.



This is a high-trust consultative sales role where you will speak directly with business owners and entrepreneurs about their tax situation and guide them into the right services.



The Sales Manager will work closely with the founder and will also oversee the firm’s Sales Development Representative (SDR).

This role is responsible for ensuring that leads move efficiently through the sales pipeline and convert into long-term clients.



Core Responsibilities



Client Closing



Conduct strategy and consultation calls with qualified prospects and convert them into clients.

Services typically sold include:

• tax strategy engagements

• tax planning services

• advisory subscriptions



Typical engagements range from $1,000 to $5,000+ per client.



Sales Pipeline Management



Manage the firm’s sales pipeline using GoHighLevel CRM.

Responsibilities include:

• monitoring inbound leads

• prioritizing high-value opportunities

• ensuring consistent follow-ups

• tracking sales pipeline metrics

The goal is to ensure no qualified lead is lost due to lack of follow-up.



Cold Lead Activation



One of the most important responsibilities of this role is reactivating dormant leads.

This includes:

• contacting older leads in our database

• re-engaging prospects who did not initially convert

• identifying opportunities for tax planning services

Many successful deals will come from reviving existing leads rather than generating new ones.



SDR Management



This role will supervise the firm’s Sales Development Representative (SDR) who handles:

• initial outreach

• appointment scheduling

• lead qualification

Responsibilities include:

• improving outreach scripts

• coaching the SDR

• improving appointment show rates

• ensuring follow-up consistency



Sales System Optimization



The Sales Manager will work with our Automation Specialist to improve the sales system.

This includes improving:

• CRM workflows

• lead routing

• appointment scheduling

• automated follow-up sequences

The goal is to build a scalable client acquisition system.



Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)



Success in this role will be measured by:

• number of strategy calls completed

• strategy call show rate

• client conversion rate

• monthly revenue generated

• reactivation of cold leads



Required Experience

Strong candidates typically have experience with:

• high-ticket consultative sales

• closing service-based offers

• CRM pipeline management

• working with international clients

Experience selling consulting, financial services, or advisory services is highly preferred.



Communication Requirements

Because this role communicates with U.S. clients, candidates must have:

• clear, neutral American accent

• excellent spoken English

• professional communication skills

Candidates must be comfortable speaking with business owners and professionals in the United States.



Leadership Responsibilities

The Sales Manager will supervise one SDR and may help expand the sales team in the future.

This role requires someone comfortable with:

• coaching tea
mbers

• improving sales scripts

• managing lead follow-up processes



Trial Period



This role begins with a 60–90 day paid trial period.

During this time, we will evaluate:

• closing ability

• communication skills

• pipeline management

• ability to improve conversion rates

Candidates who perform well during the trial period will transition into a long-term leadership role within the firm.



Compensation



Base Compensation:

$1,500 – $3,000 per month depending on experience

Performance Bonuses:

Additional bonuses based on revenue generated.

Top performers can significantly increase earnings through performance incentives.



Who This Role Is Perfect For



This role is ideal for someone who:

• enjoys consultative sales

• enjoys speaking with business owners

• understands client psychology

• enjoys building systems and processes

• wants to grow into a leadership role

This role is not ideal for someone who only wants to read scripts or perform basic call center work.



Application Instructions

To apply, please submit:

1?? Your resume

2?? A short video introduction (1–2 minutes)

3?? A brief description of your experience closing consulting or advisory services



Please begin your application with the words:



“Harvest Sales Manager.”



This helps us confirm that you read the entire job posting.

title: Medical Intake Coordinator
description:
We are seeking a detail-oriented and compassionate Medical Intake Coordinator to manage patient intake processes for our Hospice and Home Health services in California. This role serves as the primary point of contact for new referrals, patients, families, and referral partners, while also supporting light marketing and relationship-building efforts with existing referral sources.



Key Responsibilities:

1. Receive and process new hospice and home health referrals via phone, fax,
, and EMR systems.

2. Verify patient demographics, insurance eligibility, and medical documentation in compliance with HIPAA, Medicare, and California state regulations.

3. Coordinate and schedule initial assessments, admissions, and start-of-care visits with clinical staff.

4. Communicate clearly and compassionately with patients, families, physicians, hospitals, and referral partners.

5. Maintain accurate, complete, and confidential patient records in accordance with HIPAA privacy and security standards.

6. Track referral status, follow-ups, and outcomes to ensure timely admission or appropriate disposition.

7. Collaborate with clinical, billing, and administrative teams to ensure smooth transitions from intake to care delivery.

8. Provide updates to existing referral sources regarding patient status and admission outcomes.

9. Support minimal marketing activities such as maintaining referral lists, sending updates or materials to existing referral partners, and assisting with referral relationship management.

10. Assist with intake and referral reports as needed.



Requirements:

Previous experience in medical intake, hospice, home health, or healthcare coordination preferred.

Working knowledge of HIPAA compliance, patient confidentiality, and proper handling of PHI.

Strong verbal and written communication skills in English.

Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.

Ability to handle sensitive situations with professionalism, discretion, and empathy.

Proficiency with EMR systems and Microsoft Office or similar platforms.

Ability to multitask and work effectively in a fast-paced healthcare environment.



Preferred Qualifications:

Experience working with California-based hospice or home health agencies.

Familiarity with insurance verification, authorizations, physician orders, and Medicare guidelines.

Formal training or hands-on experience in HIPAA compliance and healthcare documentation standards.

Background in intake coordination, customer service, or referral management.



Monthly Rate: ?20,000

Webcam

How to Apply:

title: AI-Native Growth & CRO Operator — DTC Ecommerce (Full-Time, No Agencies)
description:
IMPORTANT: This is a full-time, dedicated position. We are not looking for agencies, freelancers juggling multiple clients, or part-time contractors. If you cannot commit full-time to one company, please do not apply.



The Company

We're a fast-scaling DTC company running two brands — one in pest control and one in sports & games — at a combined 8-figure annual run rate and growing 300–500%+ year over year. We operate with a lean, remote, global team and an AI-first culture where every tea
mber is expected to be on the cutting edge of agentic AI tools.

We don't run a traditional marketing team. We've replaced the old model of separate copywriters, designers, and CRO analysts with AI-native operators who use tools like Claude, Manus, and Cursor to produce the output of entire departments. If you're still doing everything manually and think AI is just "asking ChatGPT to rewrite something" — this isn't the role.

What This Role Actually Looks Like

You're not managing from a distance. You're hands-on, every day, building and shipping:



Designing and building landing pages on Shopify using AI tools to generate variants at speed — then testing them relentlessly

Running a constant A/B testing programme: offers, layouts, messaging, pricing, bundles, UX flows

Optimising upsells and cross-sells — testing post-purchase offers, cart upsells, bundle configurations to maximise revenue per session

Coming to the table with hypotheses, not waiting to be told what to test

Writing high-converting copy for landing pages and supporting ad copy

Working with the creative team to conceptualise new ad angles, scripts, and creative directions

Reporting on RPS (Revenue Per Session) as your north star, plus CVR, AOV, and upsell take rates



Who You Are



2–5+ years hands-on in DTC ecommerce with real CRO or growth experience (not just strategy decks)

You deploy agentic AI at a deep level — not just prompting an LLM, but building context-rich AI workflows with full product knowledge, customer avatars, awareness stages, and brand voice baked in. You should be able to describe a specific system you've built and the output it produced.

You understand direct response: offers, urgency, social proof, risk reversal, objection handling

Comfortable building and editing landing pages (Shopify, Replo, or similar)

Analytical and hypothesis-driven — you think in experiments, track results, and iterate based on data

You know what great copy looks like and can produce it (AI-assisted and expected — but you're the quality filter)

Self-starter who ships fast in a remote, async environment without needing to be managed



Bonus



Experience with A/B testing tools (VWO, Convert, Google Optimize)

Built or managed upsell funnels (ReConvert, CartHook, etc.)

Worked in sports, outdoor, home, or pest control DTC brands



Details



Full-time remote position — this is not a freelance or agency engagement

Async-friendly global team across US, Europe, and Asia

$2,000–$4,000/month depending on experience and location

Two brands: 1 pest control, 1 sports & games — combined 8-figure annual run rate

Stack: Shopify, Meta Ads, Google Ads, Asana, Slack, Claude, Notion

AI-first culture with a monthly AI challenge and the expectation that you're constantly pushing the boundaries of what's possible



How to Apply

Send us:



A brief intro on why this role excites you

1–2 examples of landing pages or CRO tests you've personally built and run (include results)

title: Product Researcher & Lister & Store Builder - Full-Time Position
description:
Full application here







Remote Ecom Operator (Dropshipping) — Run the Full Playbook End-to-End



SOPS please read before hand.















## Remote Ecom Operator (Dropshipping) — Run the Full Playbook End-to-End



We’re hiring a **hands-on, end-to-end dropshipping operator** to execute our complete operating system from **niche selection product research  supplier sourcing  store build  ads testing  fulfillment. This is not a “VA” role—this is an **owner-operator** style execution seat using a defined SOP playbook. 



### What you’ll be responsible for (end-to-end)



#### 1) Niche selection & market validation



* Use a structured niche scoring + validation process (Reddit + Google Trends + Amazon + TikTok signals) to pick testable niches. 



#### 2) Product research & competitor intelligence



* Run winning-product research using spy tools + checklists (e.g., Minea/PiPiADS + validation criteria). 

* Build product test shortlists, document competitors, extract angles/hooks, and recommend go/no-go decisions.



#### 3) Supplier sourcing, vetting & backup suppliers



* Source suppliers (CJ/AutoDS/Zendrop/Spocket/AliExpress/Alibaba as needed), vet with clear standards, and maintain **at least 2 suppliers per winning product**. 

* Confirm shipping methods/timelines and ensure policy-safe claims + realistic customer expectations.



#### 4) Shopify store build & conversion optimization



* Build/optimize Shopify store(s): theme setup, required apps, product pages, pricing, trust elements, FAQs, upsells. 

* Maintain mobile-first performance and clean customer journey (product page ? checkout ? post-purchase).



#### 5) Paid ads launch, testing & optimization (TikTok + Meta)



* Launch tests using structured testing protocols (budgeting, ad group structure, creative requirements, kill/scale rules). 

* Install pixels Day 1, monitor key metrics daily, iterate creatives fast, and pivot when data says so. 



---



## Systems & deliverables (how we work)



You will document and manage execution so work is “handoff-ready” and repeatable:



* Maintain product records, tasks, and structured research/decision packets (R0R3 style gates are a plus). 

* Keep organized folders and clear handoffs for creatives, store assets, suppliers, and performance reporting.



---



## Required experience



* Proven track record running **Shopify dropshipping or DTC testing** end-to-end (not just one slice like “product research” or “customer support”).

* Strong paid ads fundamentals: TikTok Ads + Meta Ads (testing structure, reading metrics, creative iteration, scaling decisions). 

* Comfortable owning operations: supplier coordination, fulfillment workflows, support processes, and refund/chargeback prevention. 





### Tools you are required to have and know how to use (At least 2)



Minea, PiPiADS, Meta Ad Library, Kalodata, Winning Hunter 

---



## What success looks like (core KPIs)



You will be measured on:



* Speed to launch: consistent product testing cadence (weekly/biweekly depending on scope).

* Store health: conversion rate trends, AOV improvements via upsells, refund rate control. 



---



## Role details



* **Remote / online**

* **Type:** Contract (with potential to grow into long-term operator role)

* **Hours:** Full-time 35–45 hrs/week]



  * Hourly: $1 to start

  * Performance bonus: $200 per winning product and funnel tied to net profit milestones (preferred for senior operators)



---



## How to apply



Send:



1. A short summary of your relevant wins (store results, ad spend levels, ROAS ranges, niches).

2. Links or screenshots: ad dashboards, store examples, creative examples



### Screening questions (answer in your application)



* What’s your step-by-step process to find and validate a product before spending on ads?

* Walk us through how you structure a $50/day TikTok test and what metrics d
 “kill” vs “keep.” 

* What’s your approach to reducing refunds/chargebacks in dropshipping? 

* What core
 flows do you launch first and why? 

* What tools from this stack have you personally used? 

* Full Understanding to the inital Compensation of 1USD base and 200 USD Per Validated Store/Product

* A fun fact about yourself so we know this was not written with AI

title: COO / Operations Integrator for High-Volume Dropshipping System
description:
Compensation & Incentives first month probationary period. (100 dollar increase to base everymonth thereafter)

This role is designed for someone who wants to prove themselves and earn based on performance.



Apply here: 



Base Retainer: $50 USD / week (Guaranteed for meeting minimum responsibilities).



Performance Bonus: $200 USD for every store that validates.





The Opportunity

I am building a data-driven, high-volume product validation machine, and I am looking for a COO-style Operations Integrator to own the execution.



We run a lean, volume-first testing model. We don’t test 3–5 products a month; we test 40–50. We use a structured multi-phase framework (P1 ? P2 ? P3) with clear gates, risk caps, and kill rules.



The machine is built. We need someone to fly it.



You will be the bridge between the founder and the specialist pods (research, creatives, media buying, sourcing). Your job is simple to define but hard to execute: Turn a proven validation model into a predictable, weekly execution rhythm.



Who You Are

You are NOT a VA ticking boxes. You are an operator.



You are an "Integrator": You are the calm operations brain that turns strategy into checklists, timelines, and ownership.



You think in systems: You naturally map how work moves from A ? B ? C and lock in that path.



You are data-literate: You don’t need to be a data scientist, but you can look at a report and instantly say, “We should kill this” or “We should keep testing this.”



You love upside: You prefer a role where your income scales with your results, rather than a flat hourly rate.



Core Responsibilities

You will own the day-to-day orchestration of the entire validation machine.



1. Pipeline & Capacity Management



Maintain a clear product pipeline: Idea ? Ready to Test ? P1 ? P2 ? P3 ? Scale.



Ensure we always have enough products in staging to hit our 40–50/month target.



Balance team capacity so researchers, creatives, and media buyers are never idle but never overloaded.



2. Process & SOP Execution



Enforce the 3-phase gate system. Ensure every product has the required assets before launch.



Confirm kill rules and budgets are respected (prevent emotional overspend).



Create and refine SOPs: Research handoffs, creative briefs, and media buying logging.



3. Cross-Team Coordination



Run regular check-ins with Research, Creative, and Media Buying pods.



Remove bottlenecks fast: If creatives are delayed, you escalate. If a buyer hasn’t launched, you chase. If quality drops, you reset expectations.



4. Systems & Data Hygiene



Create and own the Notion / ClickUp / Sheets structure as the single source of truth.



Maintain clean data for Products, Creatives, and Adsets so we can track validation rates and calculate bonuses accurately.



5. Continuous Improvement



Spot inefficiencies (e.g., "We need a better creative brief") and implement fixes.



Refine the validation model over time based on real results, not theory.



Requirements

2+ Years Experience: Operations, Project Management, or Integrator roles (ideally in E-commerce/Dropshipping).



Remote Team Management: Experience coordinating different pods (researchers, editors, media buyers).



Tech Stack: Strong proficiency in Notion, ClickUp/Asana, Google Sheets, and Slack.



Marketing Knowledge: Familiarity with ROAS, CPA, and AOV (you don’t need to be a media buyer, but you must speak the language).



Communication: Strong written English and relentless follow-through.



Definition: A store validates when it passes our internal criteria and moves into proper scaling.



The Upside: Since we test 40–50 products a month, the validation volume is significantly higher than low-volume models. If you run the system well, multiple validations per month are realistic, meaning your bonus will quickly outgrow the base.



Growth: As the system proves itself, we are open to discussing higher base pay and deeper profit-share arrangements.



How to Apply

Read this carefully. Applications that do not follow these instructions will be ignored.



Step 1: Review the full application details here:



Join 
 Application Channel



View Role Details on Notion



Step 2: Submit your proposal with the following:



Attention to Detail: Include the word “Integrator” somewhere in the first sentence of your proposal.



Experience: Share 1–2 specific examples where you acted as an integrator/ops manager in e-commerce. What was the monthly product or campaign volume?



Systems: Describe one system or workflow you built that made a tea
aningfully more efficient. What tools did you use? How did you measure improvement?



Scenario: If you joined tomorrow and we needed to reliably test 40–50 products per month, how would you structure the pipeline, communication, and weekly check-ins?



Confirmation: Confirm you understand and accept the compensation structure (Base + Performance Bonus).

title: Full-Ownership Media Buyer & Creative Strategist (Meta/TikTok/Google)
description:
We operate a branded wellness ecommerce store called Dayflow along with testing 15 products a week in a dropshipping fufillment model. 



Our store is functional: pages are acceptable, funnels work, and traffic flows correctly. 



What’s missing from our tests are sharp customer angles, strong creative leadership, cohesive messaging, and a single owner accountable for performance, our funnels, our testing.



This role exists to fix that gap. We are not looking for an ad button-pusher, a freelancer who waits for instructions, or someone who avoids accountability. We need a **Full-Ownership Operator**.



---



## **Your Responsibilities (End-to-End Ownership)**



### **1. Store, Funnel, & Angle Audit**



* Audit the store end-to-end to identify messaging gaps, conversion friction, and angle mismatches.

* Define real buyer avatars (Pain ? Mechanism ? Outcome). Using existing and your own research.

* Kill weak products or angles. Generic “busy professionals / self-care” thinking will be rejected.

* You will implement changes, not just suggest them.



### **2. Creative Direction, Sourcing, & Production**



* Use spy tools (e.g., Kalodata, Winning Hunter, reddit, Meta Ad library, Perplexity, Gemini—*you must already have access*) to conduct independent market research and study competitor ads.

* Source, download, and correctly identify winning creatives to either repurpose or 1-to-1 swipe for testing.

* Deliver top, middle, and bottom-of-funnel structures.

* You fully own the creative execution: d
 on hooks, formats, proof mechanisms, and CTA styles. Provide clear briefs for replacements if ads underperform.



### **3. Copywriting (Required)**



* Use AI with human audit to write all ad copy: primary text, headlines, and angle-specific messaging.

* Ensure offer/positioning language matches landing pages perfectly.

* *Note: If you outsource copy in your process, do not apply. You must understand copy as a core system component.*



### **4. Paid Ads Execution & Monitoring**



* Set up clean testing structures and launch campaigns (Meta primary; TikTok secondary).

* Iterate based on signal, kill losers decisively, and manage learning behaviors responsibly.

* Provide continuous monitoring throughout the day, responding immediately to disapprovals, broken links, or spend anomalies.



---



## **What We’re Looking For (Non-Negotiables)**



* Proven experience running DTC/dropshipping ads.

* Existing access to and proficiency with spy tools (Kalodata, Winning Hunter, etc.).

* Strong creative intuition + analytical judgment.

* Comfortable making decisions without approval loops.

* **If you need hand-holding, preset SOPs, or someone to "approve everything"—DO NOT APPLY.**



---



## **Compensation Package & "Definition of Done" (DoD)**



We operate on a brutal, contract-friendly accountability system. Compensation is strictly tied to performance and adherence to our DoD.



**1. Creative Sourcing Base:**



* **$5 per approved video + campaign Launch Milestone ** sourced/repurposed via spy tools (Expected: ~150 videos/week = **$150/week base guaranteed** for this deliverable). 

Earned *only* when the **Campaign DoD** is fully met. Hard gates include:



* **Tracking:** UTMs implemented and web events verified.

* **Funnel QA:** End-to-end walkthrough and Shopify test order completed.

* **Creative QA:** Completed QA sheet (hook clarity, compliance, promise match).

* **Build:** Clean naming conventions and documented budget strategy.

* **72-Hour Monitoring:** Continuous monitoring + active logs during the first 72 hours.

* *Fail any of these = No payout for the campaign.*



**3. Net Profit Share (2% Net Profit):**

Earned *only* while the **Ongoing Ownership DoD** is continuously met. If missed, profit share pauses for that period. Hard gates include:



* **Daily:** Active monitoring and delivery troubleshooting logs.

* **Weekly:** Updated Angle Maps, Competitor Intel Packs (10-20 ads/week), Creative Direction Briefs, Copywriting updates, and a clear Performance Report.

* **Monthly:** Comprehensive Test Archive and scaling/reallocation plans.



---



## **Trial ? Long-Term Path**



This starts as a paid trial on the existing Dayflow store. If you demonstrate strong judgment, clean execution, creative leadership, and performance improvement, you will be considered for long-term ownership of Dayflow, expanded strategic roles, and additional store budgets.



---



## **How to Apply**



To apply, please provide the following in your application:





1. **3 examples of competitor ad teardowns** (breaking down Hook / Angle / Proof / CTA).

2. Your **daily monitoring cadence** and what specifically triggers an action from you.

3. **2 Ad Copies** (Primary Text + Headline) for a “heated eye massager” using the following angles *(No medical claims)*:

* *(a)* Headache relief vibe

* *(b)* Screen fatigue vibe

4. Spy tools you have access to.

5. Methodologies for choosing creatives.

6. AI workflow for research defining custom avatars, angles, desires using PAS and breakt through advertising frameworks.

7 AI. worfklow for copywriting

8. Are you able to do basic video edits and native ad static image generation?









*(Applications missing any of these four requirements will be immediately discarded).*

description:
IMPORTANT NOTE: We will only process applicants who will submit their RESUME and a link to their VOICE RECORDING via
 to 
 is a permanent WFH employment. All government-mandated benefits are provided. We will only interview applicants who have experience. Please do not apply if you do not have a Data Center and IT industry experience.





Job Summary

The Senior Accounting Manager is responsible for managing the day-to-day accounting operations of the company and ensuring the timely execution of core accounting processes. This role will supervise the accounting team and oversee the execution of the monthly close process, reconciliations, and operational accounting workflows.



The Senior Accounting Manager will work closely with the Controller to ensure accounting activities are completed accurately and on schedule. While the Controller retains ownership of financial reporting strategy, technical accounting decisions, and executive financial oversight, this role focuses on operational execution and team management within the accounting function.



The successful candidate will possess excellent attention to detail as well as exceptional communication and analytical skills.  This individual should be comfortable in a “hands on” environment, but possess the skills necessary to grow with the Company.



Position Responsibilities to include:

Manage the day-to-day operations of the accounting department.

Supervise accounting staff responsible for Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, payroll processing support, general ledger, and other accounting activities.

Monitor team workload distribution and ensure operational continuity.

Provide coaching, guidance, and performance feedback to accounting tea
mbers.

Ensure accounting tasks and deadlines are executed according to established timelines.

Ensure an accurate and timely monthly, quarterly and year end close. Coordinate cross-functional activities in support of close requirements. Manages journal entries and conducts account reconciliations and analysis.

Review reconciliations and schedules prepared by accounting staff prior to submission to the Controller.

Produce financial statements and analyses in accordance with US GAAP.

Develop and maintain fixed asset sub-ledger.

Coordinate purchasing cycle in accordance with Company policies. 

Provide support to the external CPA firm to ensure a clean and timely year end audit.

Support budget and forecasting activities.

Assist in developing policies and internal control improvements. Recommend workflow enhancements as required.

Understands and follows payroll regulations and labor laws that impact payroll processing, ensures payroll is processed accurately and timely, and collects all necessary supporting documentation and approvals needed.

Prepare and processes the tax filings of the Company

Reviews, analyzes, and presents financial data in response to ad hoc queries from management or regulatory bodies.

 







Position Requirements:

7 to 10+ years of supervisory experience in the accounting/financial reporting areas. Experience in a growing start-up environment a plus

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or relevant work experience, CPA preferred.

Experience working in U.S.-based accounting environments.

Strong knowledge of US GAAP fundamentals.

Experience with ERP systems (NetSuite preferred).

Advanced Excel proficiency.

Experience supporting payroll and financial reporting processes.

Must have experience with cost accounting and possess thorough knowledge of job costing concepts and procedures.

Strong verbal and written communication skills.

Strong people management and team leadership skills.

Excellent interpersonal, supervisory and customer service skills.

Detail-oriented with exceptional analytical, problem solving and prioritization skills.

Ability to multi-task, work under pressure, meet deadlines and be able to thrive in a growth oriented work environment.

Position Overview

We are looking for a highly skilled, enthusiastic and self-driven Senior Operations Manager to join our team.

This individual will be responsible for managing the day to day operations and ensuring we are meeting the highest standard for customer service, as well as the smooth orchestration of order processing, commitment, and fulfillment.  



The ideal candidate will have a track record of success in a logistics role, with a natural talent for motivating cross functional teams and have a deep understanding of what it takes to build and maintain a trusted relationship with all of your business partners both internal and external. They will have a strong technical and analytical background, as well as the desire to make a significant business impact by owning and driving supply chain efficiencies to enable major business objectives. 



This position will directly oversee the Order Management and Warehouse Team.



Responsibilities



?	Supervises all shipping, receiving, and inventory control activities performed by warehouse associates.

?	Maintains department standards for productivity, reliability, and accuracy.

?	Confers with department heads to coordinate warehouse activities, such as production, sales, records control or purchasing.

?	Ensure the efficient utilization and physical condition of warehousing space by planning and implementation of new design layouts; inspecting equipment; issuing work orders for repair and requisitions for replacement.

?	Performs regular cycle counts to monitor inventory accuracy and creates action plans to address all deficiencies found in the cycle count program.

?	Reviews all shipping, receiving, and inventory discrepancies and determine the root cause.

title: Experienced Fire Alarm / Low Voltage Systems Designer (Remote | $6- $10/hour)
description:
Experienced Fire Alarm / Low Voltage Systems Designer (Remote | $6- $10/hour)



Job Description:



We are looking for a skilled and dependable Fire Alarm / Low Voltage Systems Designer to join our growing team.



This is a remote role for someone with real experience in fire alarm design, and ideally also security, CCTV, and access control systems. We need someone who can produce clean, accurate work, communicate well, and take ownership of their tasks without constant follow-up.



This is not a role for someone who only knows basic drafting. We are looking for a designer who understands system layout, code compliance, submittals, and the level of detail needed for real-world projects.



If you are experienced, responsive, and take pride in doing things right, we’d love to hear from you.



Location: Remote

   Work Type: Freelance, Part-Time, or Full-Time

   Pay: $6-10/hour USD depending on experience, skill level, and overall fit



What You’ll Be Doing



> Design fire alarm, security, CCTV, and access control systems



> Create professional drawings and submittal packages using AutoCAD



> Prepare device layouts, riser diagrams, wiring details, and design documentation



> Ensure designs comply with NFPA, IBC, IFC, and local code requirements



> Revise drawings based on project updates, AHJ comments, and internal feedback



> Coordinate with project managers, contractors, and tea
mbers when needed



What We’re Looking For



> Proven experience in fire alarm system design and/or low voltage systems design



> Strong AutoCAD skills



> Good working knowledge of NFPA 72 and related fire/life safety codes



> Strong attention to detail and accuracy



> Ability to work independently and meet deadlines



> Clear communication and good follow-through



> Someone who is responsive, reliable, and professional





Nice to Have



> NICET certification in Fire Alarm Systems

> Experience preparing complete submittal packages for AHJ approval

> Familiarity with manufacturers such as Fire-Lite, DMP, Kidde, Silent Knight, Honeywell, and similar systems





Why This Role Stands Out



We are building a team for the long term. This role is a real opportunity for the right person to grow with a company that values quality, accountability, and strong systems.



*** If you perform well, there may be room for more hours, a long-term role, and future leadership opportunities.***



How to Apply



We receive a lot of applications, so we only consider candidates who follow instructions carefully.

Please complete all of the steps below:



>> Send your resume

>> Write a short paragraph explaining why you are a strong fit for this role

>> Answer this question in 2–3 sentences:

      If you are designing a fire alarm system and the AHJ requests additional devices after your initial submittal, how would you handle the change?



>> Send a 2-minute video introduction telling us:

*who you are

*your experience in fire alarm / low voltage system design

*what types of projects you’ve worked on

*why you think you would be a good fit for this role



Upload the video using Google Drive or Loom and include the link in your application



Include your availability:

      --->> Freelance, Part-Time, or Full-Time



Use this exact subject line in your application:

 Designer Application – [Your Name]



At the very end of your application, include this exact phrase:

 I want to synergize with you



Important!!

Applications that do not follow the instructions above will not be prioritized.

We are looking for someone who is not only technically capable, but also attentive, professional, and serious about the opportunity.

If that sounds like you, we’d be glad to review your application.

title: Experienced Part/Full-Time Bookkeeper /Accountant (Remote)
description:
Please Read Carefully



To be considered for this position, you must carefully follow all application instructions.



Applications that do not follow the instructions or appear to be AI-generated will not be considered.

A short video introduction is required as part of the application process.



Please read the Application Instructions section at the bottom before applying.



About the Role



We are a fire and security contracting and engineering company seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Bookkeeper / Staff Accountant to maintain accurate financial records, organize documentation, and support smooth business operations.



This role is ideal for someone who takes ownership of their work, values structure and accuracy, and wants to grow into a leadership role in finance.



This is more than a bookkeeping role. It is an opportunity to become a key financial operator inside a growing company.



Key Responsibilities



Financial Management

Manage QuickBooks Online, including creating and sending invoices, tracking payments, and reconciling accounts

Maintain accurate financial records and ensure books are CPA-ready for tax filing and review

Process invoices, unpaid invoices, estimates, and job documentation

Review job completion forms to ensure accurate billing

Operational Accounting Support

Upload invoices and track payments in Service Channel

Monitor receivables and follow up on unpaid invoices

Ensure financial records are complete and properly documented

Documentation & Organization

Organize and audit Google Drive files using standardized naming conventions

Ensure paperwork and documents are routed to the correct stakeholders

Maintain strict organizational standards across all financial documentation

Manage and track insurance documentation

Systems & Process Support

Help create and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)

Track and maintain relevant KPIs

Support internal systems and documentation using Notion



Requirements



5+ years of bookkeeping or accounting experience

Expert proficiency in QuickBooks Online (multi-entity experience preferred)

Strong experience with Google Sheets or Excel (formulas, pivot tables, reporting)

Proficiency in Google Workspace (Docs, Drive, Gmail, Calendar)

Comfortable using Notion for documentation and task tracking

Familiarity with Zoho CRM is highly preferred

Experience with Service Channel is a plus

Strong written and spoken English communication skills

Experience creating SOP documentation

Ability to track and maintain KPIs and operational metrics



Personal Traits



Highly organized and detail-oriented

Reliable and proactive

Independent worker with strong problem-solving skills

Strong sense of ownership and accountability



Position Details



Location: Remote (work from anywhere)

Schedule Options

      Part-Time

20 hours per week

      Full-Time

40 hours per week



Candidates must be available during U.S. business hours (8:00 AM – 4:00 PM Eastern Time) and reachable during that window.



Growth & Career Path



We are building a long-term team, and the right candidate will have opportunities to grow within the organization.

Potential growth path includes:

Transition to full-time employment

Leadership opportunity as Head of Finance Department

Long-term role within a fast-growing, tech-enabled contracting and engineering company

This role is ideal for someone looking for more than just a job, but a long-term career with increasing responsibility.



Application Instructions (Read Carefully)



To apply for this role, you must complete all five steps below.



Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



Step 1

Write a short paragraph explaining why you believe you are a good fit for this role.



Step 2

Answer the following question in 2–3 sentences:



If you are given five invoices to process, but one of them is missing supporting documents, what would you do?



Step 3

Use the following subject line in your
 application:



Bookkeeper Application – [Your Name]



Step 4

At the very end of your application, include the exact phrase:



“I want to synergize with you.”



Step 5



Submit a 2-minute video explaining your experience in:



Bookkeeping



Staff accounting



Financial controlling



You may upload the video using Google Drive or Loom and include the link in your application.
